summary:
Game Day 1 is in the books and the World Cup is officially alive. Mexico came out swinging against South Africa, grabbing a 1-0 win that was probably more comfortable than the scoreline suggests. South Africa worked hard but looked every bit the Tier 3 team they are — which is great news for nobody who drew them.

Mexico look decent. Compact defensively, dangerous on the counter. Their Tier 1 billing might be justified after all, which is either brilliant or devastating depending on whose squad you're looking at right now.

For the Wooden Spoon race, South Africa shipped one in Game 1 — not a disaster, but if that becomes a theme across the group stage someone is going to be counting goals conceded with increasing dread. The Beautiful Loser conversation is already open for business.

One game, one result, 103 to go. The Avalanche has begun.

Early mover of the day: whoever has Mexico. Early sufferer: whoever has South Africa and is already doing the maths.
